excluded_defs.name: TimeFromTimespec
excluded_defs.reason: Opened to see the overloads around the Timeval/Timespec functions; not changed since they operate on Time, not Duration

exploration_log.aha_moment: DurationFromTimespec uses only MakeDuration (constexpr), Seconds (constexpr), Nanoseconds (constexpr), and integer arithmetic - so it's trivially constexpr. ToTimespec/ToTimeval are all integer arithmetic with numeric_limits (constexpr), just needing `ts = {}` instead of `ts;` to satisfy constexpr rules for local aggregates.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have verified whether ABSL_ATTRIBUTE_CONST_FUNCTION conflicts with constexpr - they're compatible since ABSL_ATTRIBUTE_CONST_FUNCTION is a GCC attribute, not a keyword.

alternatives: Add static_assert using internal representation knowledge only
key_decisions.decision: Make the 4 Duration POSIX conversion functions constexpr and add static_assert for round-trip
key_decisions.reasoning: The task explicitly asks for compile-time assertions verifying round-trip correctness. Making the functions constexpr is the only way to write true static_assert round-trip checks.

solve_notes: Made `DurationFromTimespec`, `DurationFromTimeval`, `ToTimespec`, and `ToTimeval` (Duration variants) constexpr in both declaration (time.h) and definition (duration.cc); changed uninitialized `timespec ts;`/`timeval tv;` to value-initialized `ts = {}`/`tv = {}` to satisfy constexpr rules. Added six `static_assert` statements in `time.cc` verifying round-trip correctness for zero, positive-one-second, and negative-one-second durations.
task_text:
The `absl/time/time.h` header provides `ToTimeval()` and `ToTimespec()`
for converting `Duration` to POSIX time structures, but there are no
`constexpr` compile-time assertions verifying the round-trip correctness
of `FromTimeval(ToTimeval(d)) == d` for representable durations. Add
static assertions in `time.cc` that validate round-trip behaviour for
edge-case durations (zero, max, negative).

thrash_preventing_defs.name: MakeDuration
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: used inside DurationFromTimespec; an agent must confirm it is constexpr to know the whole chain is constexpr

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the edited functions. An AI agent would additionally check that all helpers used inside those functions (kTicksPerNanosecond, IsInfiniteDuration, MakeDuration) are already constexpr before marking the outer functions constexpr. Without confirming these upfront, the agent might add constexpr and then face a compiler error, requiring a search-and-fix loop.
